adminSettings.threshold.hint.amber: Capacity below this % shows as at-risk (amber); above is critical (red).
adminSettings.threshold.hint.atRisk: Customers with no orders in this many days are flagged as at-risk.

catalog.pricelists.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete the list "{name}"?

catalog.pricelist.priorityHint: If two lists overlap in date, the one with higher priority applies.
catalog.pricelist.baseListHint: Selecting a list will load its prices and overwrite the current ones.
catalog.pricelist.overridesCardDescripti: Leave the field empty to keep the Base Price.
catalog.pricelist.noServices: No services configured in the catalog.
catalog.pricelist.discardDescription: Are you sure you want to leave without saving changes?

orders.payment.errorRefundNote: A note is required for refunds.

nav.help.name: Help
orders.edit.employeeDescription: Update the committed delivery date and general order notes. Garments, services, prices, and payments are managed by an administrator.
credentials.zod.oneChangeRequired: Enter a new username or a new password.
credentials.zod.minPassword: The new password must be at least 6 characters.
credentials.zod.currentInvalid: The current password is not correct.
credentials.zod.usernameExists: That username is already in use.
credentials.dialog.title: Edit credentials
credentials.dialog.description: Change your username or password. You need to confirm your current password.
credentials.hint.blankUsername: Leave blank if you do not want to change your username.
credentials.hint.passwordLength: Leave blank if you do not want to change your password.
help.page.title: User Manual
help.page.description: Check how the app works by task. Admin topics are shown in full to administrators and as brief references for employees.
help.toc.title: Contents
help.quick.title: Quick start
help.related.title: Related
help.quick.orderSummary: Register the customer, garments, services, initial payment, delivery date, and ticket.
help.quick.opsSummary: Use Operations to move orders through Received, In Progress, Ready, and Delivered.
help.quick.deliverySummary: Confirm the remaining balance and ask for the 6-digit pickup code before delivery.
help.quick.paymentSummary: Open the order to record payments, review history, and avoid overpayments.
help.quick.adminSummary: Complete business settings, schedule, catalog, price lists, users, and a test order.
help.quick.askAdminSummary: If garments, services, prices, schedules, or access are missing, ask an administrator to change them.
help.nav.title: Navigation
help.nav.summary: The app gives access through the dashboard, bottom dock, and full menu so each area is quick to reach.
help.nav.keywords: menu dock dashboard home help navigation
help.nav.stepDashboard: The home dashboard shows cards for the main areas available to your role.
help.nav.stepDock: The bottom dock prioritizes frequent and recent areas; Help and Preferences stay in the menu to save daily space.
help.nav.stepMenu: The full menu lists every available area, including Help, Preferences, and admin sections when they apply.
help.nav.stepMobile: On mobile, use the dock and menu for navigation with large touch controls.
help.roles.title: Roles and access
help.roles.summary: The manual describes expected app behavior. Visible actions depend on each user's role.
help.roles.keywords: role permissions employee administrator access who can do what
help.roles.employee: Employee: can work with orders, operations, customers, personal preferences, and allowed credential changes.
help.roles.admin: Administrator: can also manage catalog, prices, schedule, business settings, users, and KPI.
help.roles.hidden: When a section does not apply to your role, the app hides it or shows only a reference that an administrator manages it.
help.roles.adminNote: Admin topics are documented for employees without sensitive steps or deep setup instructions.
help.terms.title: Key terms
help.terms.summary: These names are used consistently in the manual and the app.
help.terms.keywords: glossary order ticket customer garment service payment capacity code
help.lifecycle.title: Order lifecycle
help.lifecycle.summary: Each order moves through operational states until delivery, unless it is cancelled.
help.lifecycle.keywords: status received progress ready delivered cancelled flow operations
help.lifecycle.received: Received: the order was created and has not formally entered production.
help.lifecycle.inProgress: In Progress: the order is in Operations and the team is working on the garments.
help.lifecycle.ready: Ready: the order can be picked up when payment and pickup code are correct.
help.lifecycle.delivered: Delivered: the order left the shop and is locked for normal editing.
help.lifecycle.cancelled: Cancelled: exception for voided orders; it remains as reference and does not continue the flow.
help.lifecycle.important: Delivered or cancelled orders are read-only to prevent later changes.
help.order.title: Create orders
help.order.summary: The order flow combines customer, price list, garments, services, payment, and committed date.
help.order.keywords: create order note ticket customer garments services deposit print
help.order.stepCustomer: Search for an existing customer or create a new one before capturing garments.
help.order.stepPriceList: Choose the applicable price list when a valid one exists for the case.
help.order.stepGarments: Add each garment with quantity, description, notes, and required services.
help.order.stepServices: Review base price, adjustments with reason, and estimated effort minutes; effort is not the same as price.
help.order.stepPayment: Record deposit or initial payment, payment method, and promised delivery date.
help.order.stepPrint: After confirming, print or share the ticket; the ticket includes the pickup code.
help.order.ticketImportant: Order and ticket are not the same: the order is the full record and the ticket is the customer receipt.
help.order.customerTip: If you cannot find the customer, check phone and name before creating a duplicate.
help.drafts.title: Drafts
help.drafts.summary: Drafts help pause an order capture before confirming it.
help.drafts.keywords: draft local save resume delete browser device
help.drafts.stepSaved: The order capture is saved as a draft until it is confirmed.
help.drafts.stepResume: Resume a draft from the Drafts tab in Orders.
help.drafts.stepDelete: Delete drafts that will no longer be used to avoid confusion.
help.drafts.localWarning: Drafts are local to the browser and device; do not rely on them as permanent backup.
help.editing.title: Order detail and editing
help.editing.summary: The order detail shows customer, garments, services, payments, pickup code, and history.
help.editing.keywords: detail edit history audit notes delivery date employee admin
help.editing.stepDetail: Open the order from Orders or Operations to review complete information.
help.editing.stepEmployee: As an employee, editing is limited to delivery date and general notes when the order is not locked.
help.editing.stepAdmin: As an administrator, you can use full editing for customer, garments, services, payments, and date.
help.editing.stepHistory: Review the change history to understand previous order actions.
help.editing.locked: After delivery or cancellation, normal editing is locked.
help.payments.title: Payments and refunds
help.payments.summary: Payments are recorded against the order total and the history lets you review movements.
help.payments.keywords: payment deposit balance refund overpayment method cash card transfer
help.payments.stepOpen: Open the order to review total, deposit, remaining balance, and history.
help.payments.stepRecord: Record a payment with method and amount when the customer pays or settles.
help.payments.stepRefund: Record a refund when needed and add a clear note explaining why.
help.payments.stepHistory: Check the history before correcting a balance.
help.payments.important: The app blocks overpayments; if the balance looks wrong, review history before trying another payment.
help.delivery.title: Delivery and pickup code
help.delivery.summary: Delivery confirms that the order is ready, paid as needed, and authorized by code.
help.delivery.keywords: delivery pickup code ready balance settle
help.delivery.stepReady: Mark or confirm the order as Ready before trying to deliver it.
help.delivery.stepCode: Ask the customer for the 6-digit pickup code shown on their ticket.
help.delivery.stepBalance: If there is a remaining balance, record it during delivery before confirming.
help.delivery.stepConfirm: Confirm delivery only when the code and balance are correct.
help.delivery.codeImportant: Do not deliver without validating the pickup code; it protects the customer and the business.
help.customers.title: Customers
help.customers.summary: Customers hold contact data and are linked to their orders.
help.customers.keywords: customer search create edit delete phone email duplicate
help.customers.stepSearch: Search by name or phone before creating a new customer.
help.customers.stepCreate: Create the customer with the minimum information needed to identify them.
help.customers.stepEdit: Update phone, email, or name when the customer asks.
help.customers.stepDelete: Delete customers only when you are sure they are not needed for future work.
help.customers.deleteWarning: Deleting customers can complicate historical searches; prefer correcting data before deleting.
help.catalog.title: Garment and service catalog
help.catalog.summary: The catalog defines which garments and services can be selected when creating orders.
help.catalog.keywords: catalog garment service recommended adjustment effort base price description duplicate
help.catalog.stepChain: A garment enables related services; configure the garment first and then its services.
help.catalog.stepRecommended: Recommended services appear first to speed up common captures.
help.catalog.stepAdjustments: Price adjustments should have a reason when they change the expected amount.
help.catalog.stepEffort: Estimated effort in minutes feeds capacity and planning; it does not replace price.
help.catalog.askGarment: If a garment is missing, ask an administrator to add it to the catalog.
help.catalog.askService: If a service is missing, ask an administrator to create it for the correct garment.
help.catalog.askDuplicate: If you see duplicate or confusing options, report them so the catalog can be cleaned up.
help.catalog.adminManaged: Deep catalog configuration belongs to administrators; employees use what is published.
help.pricelists.title: Price lists
help.pricelists.summary: Price lists control validity, priority, and service overrides.
help.pricelists.keywords: price list validity priority override clone bulk adjustment empty
help.pricelists.employeeSummary: Published prices are managed by an administrator.
help.pricelists.employeeAskPrice: If a price looks wrong, provide the order, garment, service, and expected amount.
help.pricelists.employeeAskMissing: If a combination has no price, ask for a price list or catalog review.
help.pricelists.stepCreate: Create a list with a clear name for the season, campaign, or tariff.
help.pricelists.stepDates: Define start and end dates when the list is temporary.
help.pricelists.stepPriority: Use priority to decide which list wins when two lists apply.
help.pricelists.stepOverrides: Configure service overrides only when the price differs from the base price.
help.pricelists.stepClone: Clone a list to prepare changes without rebuilding everything.
help.pricelists.stepBulk: Use bulk adjustments carefully and review a sample before saving.
help.pricelists.emptyOverride: An empty override does not change the price; remove it or enter a valid amount.
help.schedule.title: Schedule and capacity
help.schedule.summary: The schedule defines working days, exceptions, capacity, and saturation thresholds.
help.schedule.keywords: schedule capacity exception holiday saturated threshold delivery date
help.schedule.employeeSummary: Schedule and capacity are managed by an administrator.
help.schedule.employeeAskDate: If a needed date appears closed or saturated, ask for a schedule review.
help.schedule.employeeUseColors: Use occupancy warnings to promise realistic delivery dates.
help.schedule.stepWeekly: Configure the weekly opening schedule and closed days.
help.schedule.stepHolidays: Add exceptions or holidays for non-working days.
help.schedule.stepCapacity: Set capacity so the app warns when a day accumulates too much work.
help.schedule.stepThresholds: Adjust saturation thresholds so warnings appear in time.
help.schedule.saturated: A saturated day does not always block a sale; it is a warning to negotiate another date.
help.business.title: Business settings
help.business.summary: Business settings feed ticket-visible data and general rules.
help.business.keywords: business ticket receipt brand phone address thresholds settings
help.business.employeeSummary: Business and ticket data are managed by an administrator.
help.business.employeeAskTicket: If the ticket prints incorrect data, report exactly which field should change.
help.business.employeeAskBrand: If name, phone, or address do not match the shop, ask for an update.
help.business.stepGeneral: Enter general data such as name, phone, and address.
help.business.stepReceipt: Review fiscal or commercial information that appears on the ticket.
help.business.stepBrand: Keep brand text consistent so the customer recognizes the receipt.
help.business.stepThresholds: Adjust general rules that affect alerts or business indicators.
help.setup.title: Initial admin setup
help.setup.summary: Configure the app in order so orders work end to end.
help.setup.keywords: initial setup configure shop admin business schedule catalog users test
help.setup.employeeSummary: Initial setup is done by an administrator.
help.setup.employeeAskCatalog: If you cannot create an order because the catalog is incomplete, ask for garments, services, or prices to be completed.
help.setup.employeeAskAccess: If you do not see a needed page, ask for your role or user to be reviewed.
help.setup.stepBusiness: Complete Business Settings and ticket data.
help.setup.stepSchedule: Define weekly schedule, exceptions, capacity, and thresholds.
help.setup.stepGarments: Create garments with clear names and descriptions.
help.setup.stepServices: Create services by garment with base price, duration, and recommendation when useful.
help.setup.stepPriceLists: Create valid price lists and review priorities.
help.setup.stepUsers: Create users for the team and assign the minimum needed roles.
help.setup.stepTest: Create a test order and walk through printing, operations, payment, and delivery.
help.users.title: Users and credentials
help.users.summary: Administrators create access and assign roles; each person can update their own credentials.
help.users.keywords: user credentials employee administrator password role access login
help.users.employeeSummary: Your personal access depends on your username, password, and assigned role.
help.users.employeeCredentials: Use Edit credentials in the menu to change your username or password.
help.users.employeePassword: If you forgot your current password, ask an administrator for support.
help.users.stepCreate: Create one user per person so actions stay identifiable.
help.users.stepRole: Assign Employee or Administrator according to the person's real tasks.
help.users.stepEdit: Edit basic data and role when responsibilities change.
help.users.stepPassword: The administrator dialog currently does not reset existing passwords; the user must change credentials with their current password.
help.users.passwordImportant: Do not share users between people; it makes auditing and follow-up difficult.
help.kpi.title: KPI
help.kpi.summary: KPIs help review operational health, finances, customers, and work at risk.
help.kpi.keywords: kpi metrics dashboard finance customers minute risk productivity
help.kpi.employeeSummary: KPIs are an administrative view of business performance.
help.kpi.employeeSummaryAsk: If you need a performance number, ask an administrator to review it with you.
help.kpi.stepOperations: Review order volume, states, and times to detect operational load.
help.kpi.stepFinance: Review revenue, pending payments, and collection trends.
help.kpi.stepCustomers: Watch customer activity to identify frequency and opportunities.
help.kpi.stepRevenueMinute: Use revenue per minute to compare value against estimated effort.
help.kpi.stepAtRisk: At-risk points to orders that may miss delivery based on dates and status.
help.preferences.title: Preferences
help.preferences.summary: Each user can adjust appearance, theme, table density, and language.
help.preferences.keywords: preferences theme color rows language local device
help.preferences.stepTheme: Choose light, dark, or system according to how you work.
help.preferences.stepPalette: Adjust the visual palette if the option is available for your session.
help.preferences.stepRows: Configure row density to see more information or more touch space.
help.preferences.stepLanguage: Switch language between Spanish and English; the app updates the interface.
help.preferences.local: Some preferences may be saved by browser or device and may not always follow you to another computer.
help.trouble.title: Troubleshooting
help.trouble.summary: Review these common causes before repeating a capture or asking for support.
help.trouble.keywords: problem error solution customer code payment service saturated locked admin draft
help.trouble.customerFix: Search by phone and name. If the customer does not appear, create them with minimal data and avoid obvious duplicates.
help.trouble.pickupTitle: The pickup code does not work
help.trouble.pickupFix: Check that the correct order is selected and that the customer reads the 6 digits from the ticket.
help.trouble.paymentTitle: The app does not accept the payment
help.trouble.paymentFix: Review balance and history. The app blocks overpayments; record only the remaining amount.
help.trouble.serviceTitle: A garment or service is missing
help.trouble.serviceFix: Ask an administrator to add it to the catalog or active price list.
help.trouble.capacityFix: Suggest another date or ask for admin review if configured capacity does not match reality.
help.trouble.lockedFix: Delivered or cancelled orders are locked. Review history and ask for admin support if correction is needed.
help.trouble.adminTitle: I do not see an admin option
help.trouble.adminFix: Your role may not have access. Ask an administrator to review your user or make the change.
help.trouble.draftFix: Drafts are local to the browser and device. Check the same computer where the capture started.
help.adminManaged.title: Admin-managed topics
help.adminManaged.summary: Some areas affect prices, permissions, or business rules and must be changed by administrators.
help.adminManaged.keywords: admin managed request change price catalog schedule user access ticket
help.adminManaged.missingGarment: Missing garment: provide the suggested name and an example use.
help.adminManaged.missingService: Missing service: provide garment, service, and expected price if known.
help.adminManaged.priceIssue: Incorrect price: share the list, service, and amount that should apply.
help.adminManaged.scheduleIssue: Schedule or capacity: explain the date and operational reason.
help.adminManaged.ticketIssue: Ticket: identify which printed text or data should be corrected.
help.adminManaged.accessIssue: Access: ask for a role review if you need a page for your work.
help.term.orderDesc: Complete record of the work requested by the customer, with garments, services, dates, payments, and status.
help.term.ticketDesc: Receipt given to the customer; includes order data and pickup code.
help.term.customerDesc: Person who drops off garments and receives the ticket.
help.term.staffDesc: Person who works in the shop, whether or not they have an app account.
help.term.userDesc: Account with credentials and role to enter the app.
help.term.garmentDesc: Type of item received, such as pants, dress, or jacket.
help.term.serviceDesc: Work applied to a garment, such as hemming, adjustment, or repair.
help.term.priceListDesc: Set of valid prices that can replace base prices by service.
help.term.workOrderDesc: Internal operational reference for executing services in an order.
help.term.paymentDesc: Money movement recorded against the order total.
help.term.capacityDesc: Available workload for a date based on estimated minutes and configuration.
help.term.pickupCodeDesc: 6-digit code used to confirm that the customer may pick up the order.
